topic: complex-valued diffusion denoiser with phase interaction layer for OFDM channel estimation

strengths: Excellent coverage with 15 relevant papers meeting the minimum requirement
Strong representation of recent work (2023-2025) including very recent 2025 papers
All papers appear to be real with valid arXiv IDs that can be verified
Comprehensive gap analysis identifying 5 distinct research opportunities
Well-structured taxonomy organizing papers into meaningful categories
Complete BibTeX entries with all required fields (author, title, journal, year)
High relevance scores (0.6-0.95) showing focused literature selection
Good mix of foundational work (DDPM) and domain-specific applications
weaknesses: Limited coverage of complex-valued neural network papers - only 3-4 directly address CVNNs
Missing some key references on phase-aware neural architectures
Gap analysis could be more specific about implementation challenges
Some papers have tangential relevance (e.g., medical imaging CNN at 0.6 relevance)
Could benefit from more papers specifically on OFDM channel estimation with neural networks
1.feedback: This is a solid literature review that provides good coverage of the intersection between diffusion models and wireless communications. To strengthen it further: 1) Add 2-3 more papers specifically on complex-valued neural networks for signal processing, 2) Include papers on phase-aware architectures if they exist, 3) Consider removing the medical imaging paper (lowest relevance) and replacing with more relevant work, 4) The gap analysis is strong but could be more specific about why these gaps matter for practical implementations.

strengths: Excellent novelty - the Phase Interaction Layer with wave-function-inspired parameterization ψ(z) = a(z)·exp(iφ(z)) is genuinely novel and well-motivated for wireless channels
High feasibility - detailed architecture specs (~180K parameters) with realistic training time estimates (45-60 min on M4)
Clear significance - addresses a real gap in complex-valued diffusion models for wireless communications with concrete performance targets (3-5 dB improvement)
Well-defined scope - explicitly states what's in/out of scope, focusing on SISO OFDM channel estimation
Three diverse candidate ideas showing thorough exploration of the design space
Strong connection to identified literature gaps with opportunity score 0.95
weaknesses: Limited justification for T=500 diffusion steps - seems high for real-time applications
No discussion of inference time complexity which is critical for practical deployment
Phase Interaction Layer description could be more precise about how amplitude and phase heads interact before Euler composition
Expected 3-5 dB improvement seems optimistic without preliminary experiments
2.feedback: The ideation is strong and ready to proceed to problem formulation. To improve: (1) Consider reducing diffusion steps for faster inference, (2) Add inference time estimates alongside training time, (3) Clarify if there's any coupling/interaction between amplitude and phase predictions beyond separate heads, (4) Temper performance claims or mark as 'targeted' improvements. The core idea of complex-valued diffusion with Phase Interaction Layer is novel and well-scoped for the constraints.

3.feedback:
I'll review the Problem Formulation stage output according to the specified criteria.

strengths: Honest acknowledgment that proposed method underperforms baselines
Comprehensive analysis across multiple SNR values with clear tabulated results
Good interpretation of SNR-dependent behavior and phase interaction effects
Figures are properly formatted with correct scales (NMSE in dB, BER on log scale)
Clear identification of diffusion model limitations for channel estimation
Thoughtful discussion of why the Phase Interaction Layer doesn't provide expected benefits
weaknesses: Figure generation code is just a description, not actual matplotlib/seaborn code
No explicit mention of 3.5" width requirement for figures
Analysis could better connect back to the original hypothesis about quantum-inspired phase rotations
Missing discussion of computational complexity trade-offs
Could provide more concrete suggestions for future improvements
5.feedback: The analysis is scientifically honest and thorough, correctly interpreting negative results as valid findings. The narrative provides good insights into why the Phase Interaction Layer fails to improve performance. To strengthen this section: (1) Include actual Python code for figure generation with explicit width=3.5 inches, (2) Add a brief computational complexity comparison table, (3) Expand on alternative architectures that might better exploit phase relationships. The current analysis is publication-quality in terms of scientific rigor, just needs minor technical improvements for figure generation.

strengths: Complete IEEE two-column format with proper structure and all required sections
Coherent flow from introduction through methodology to results and conclusion
Consistent notation throughout - symbols match the formulation stage (K=64, P=16, etc.)
All citations have matching BibTeX entries in the bibliography
Figure references are properly formatted with \ref{fig:X} and matching \label{fig:X}
Honest reporting of negative results - acknowledges that proposed method doesn't outperform baselines
Well-structured abstract that summarizes key contributions and findings
Appropriate length at approximately 5-6 pages
Mathematical equations are properly numbered and formatted
Clear experimental setup with all parameters specified
weaknesses: Figure inclusion uses placeholder paths (figures/fig1_nmse.pdf) without actual figure generation
Title uses 'Quantum-Inspired Phase Rotation Gate' which differs from 'Phase Interaction Layer' used in previous stages
Some terminology inconsistency - switches between 'Phase Interaction Layer' and 'Quantum Phase Rotation Gate'
Table formatting could be improved - the NMSE comparison table is quite wide
Missing actual compilation to PDF - only source provided
Author block shows 'Anonymous Authors' placeholder
Some verbose sections could be condensed for better use of space
6.feedback: The manuscript is well-written and publication-ready with minor revisions. The paper honestly reports that the proposed diffusion model approach doesn't outperform baselines, which is scientifically valid. To improve: (1) Maintain consistent terminology throughout - choose either 'Phase Interaction Layer' or 'Quantum Phase Rotation Gate' and stick with it, (2) Generate actual figures or provide clear instructions for figure generation, (3) Consider condensing the related work section to make room for more analysis of why the method underperforms, (4) Add a brief computational complexity comparison in the results section, (5) Fill in actual author information before submission. The paper successfully presents a negative result in a constructive way that contributes to the field's understanding.
